title: microlepia rheophila
intro: Microlepia rheophila, a type of fern native to Southeast Asia, is a plant species known for its medicinal properties and uses. The plant is traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Microlepia rheophila include reducing fever and inflammation, improving digestion, and alleviating pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions.
